title: Online Etiquette
subtitle: Think Before You Click & Type!
overview: This session helps students understand how to behave respectfully and safely online. Students learn about polite communication, responsible posting, respecting privacy, avoiding cyberbullying, and becoming kind digital citizens.

images._note: Add screenshots of chats, online classes, emails, social media examples, and classroom activities.

keyTopics: Think Before You Type — Words Matter Online
Respect Others During Online Chats & Classes
Do Not Share Personal Information Publicly
Use Polite Language in Emails and Chats
Respect Privacy — Ask Before Posting Photos
Strong Passwords and Safe Online Accounts

activity.title: Polite or Rude? Online Chat Activity
steps: Show students examples of online chat messages
Ask students to identify polite and rude behavior
Create classroom online etiquette rules together
Challenge: Make a 'Think Before You Post' poster

options: Good manners and respectful behavior online
quiz.explanation: Online Etiquette means behaving politely and responsibly on the internet.

quiz.q: What should you do before posting online?
options: Think if it is kind, safe, and respectful
quiz.explanation: Always THINK before posting anything online.

quiz.q: Which of these is an example of polite online behavior?
options: Typing in ALL CAPS to shout
quiz.explanation: Respectful communication is part of good online etiquette.
Cyberbullying means using technology to hurt or bully others.

quiz.q: Should you share your password with friends?
quiz.explanation: Passwords protect your accounts and should remain private.
Everything you do online can leave a digital footprint.

quiz.q: Why is typing in ALL CAPS usually avoided?
quiz.explanation: ALL CAPS can seem rude because it looks like shouting.

quiz.q: What should you do if someone is rude online?
options: Tell a trusted adult or teacher
quiz.explanation: Report harmful behavior to a trusted adult.

quiz.q: What is safe to share online?
quiz.explanation: Avoid sharing private or personal details online.

quiz.q: What should you do before posting someone’s photo online?
quiz.explanation: Respect privacy and ask before sharing photos.

quiz.q: Which is a good online class behavior?
quiz.explanation: Good online manners help everyone learn better.

quiz.q: What kind of password is strong?
options: A mix of letters, numbers, and symbols
quiz.explanation: Strong passwords are harder for others to guess.

quiz.q: Why should we respect others online?
options: To create a safe and friendly internet
quiz.explanation: Respect makes the online world safer and kinder.

quiz.q: Which action is NOT good online etiquette?
quiz.explanation: Sharing fake information is irresponsible behavior.

quiz.q: What should students remember while using social media?
options: Internet posts can stay online permanently
quiz.explanation: Online posts can stay forever, so always post responsibly.
